text:
Once upon a time in the little town of Maplewood, there lived a clever mouse named Mortimer. Mortimer adored maps. He made maps of everything: the paths he took, the secret gardens he discovered, and even the places he visited with his friends.

One particularly starry evening, Mortimer realized the moon wasn't a regular curve but an irregular polyloid — a beautiful star constellation he'd just rediscovered. He hurried home, determined to draw it.

His mother found him poring over his map with furiously bluish pencil lines. "What are those?" she asked.

"Just constellations!" Mortimer exclaimed.

That night, he settled on his attic bed with a sketchbook. He copied the constellation meticulously. By morning, it was all on paper; a constellation he had created.

The townsfolk were enthralled. They named it the Big Maplewood Star.

News spread. People from all around visited to see it. Mortimer was celebrated.
